mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate above prior 12-month average keeps discount rates high, compressing equity valuations and producing below-median S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains near cycle highs.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33 in 2024-06-01 vintage, near cycle high
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-FEDFUNDS periods show forward returns statistically indistinguishable from full-sample average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by at least 0.5 percentage points from its trailing 12-month minimum (a Sahm-rule-style trigger), it signals recession onset, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the subsequent quarter as earnings expectations deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% in 2024-06-01, up from cycle lows near 3.4%
failure_condition: In periods where the 0.5pp trigger fires, forward 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or average triggered-period return is not significantly below unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of high nominal FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation implies rising real interest rates, which tightens financial conditions and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY change decelerating for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 5.33% with CPIAUCSL disinflation trend into mid-2024
failure_condition: Interaction term (real rate proxy) shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone (delta Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 3-year windows, or joint-trigger periods show non-negative average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) over a 6-month period signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter, but only when the economy is already in a late-cycle expansion phase with restrictive monetary policy.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from 1316.0 revised to 1277.0 in 2024-05-01 vintage
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: During HOUST-decline trigger episodes occurring outside high-FEDFUNDS regimes, forward 60-day returns show no significant negative bias (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears when tested outside late-cycle windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as markets price in continued production strength.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 100.863 vs vintage 103.1168 in 2024-05-01
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and forward 5-day returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no short-horizon predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS), defined as month-over-month gains falling below 100,000 jobs, signals labor market cooling that leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as growth expectations are revised downward, particularly when the unemployment rate is simultaneously above 4%.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised 157695 vs vintage 158638 in 2024-06-01; UNRATE at 4.1%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce forward 20-day returns that are non-negative in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction adds no incremental Rank IC over PAYEMS alone (delta < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) above its trailing 6-month trend indicates robust consumer spending that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised 692774 vs vintage 703088 in 2024-05-01, indicating continued nominal growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS trend deviation and forward 20-day returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or above-trend RSAFS periods do not outperform below-trend periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of restrictive monetary policy (FEDFUNDS above 4.5%), a deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% annualized signals that tight policy is successfully slowing demand, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as recession risk rises; in accommodative regimes the same GDP deceleration has no consistent directional effect.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 2% while FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised to 23082.119 from vintage 22758.752 for 2024-01-01; FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
failure_condition: Under the joint trigger, forward 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the negative-direction effect fails to replicate across at least two distinct restrictive-policy episodes in the historical record.
